If you take one of your three awareness’, are you not taking a perspective that can look like this? When you are in this isolated perspective, how do you see and interpret the other parts of yourself? Are you even looking in that direction? If you take the forefront of this diagram as representing one available perspective, is there more than one way you can interpret the rest of yourself? Can you see a view of yourself as duality from this diagram? As if the other two parts of you were to combine as a Not You for example? Can you see a view of yourself as trinity? A perspective that looks more like an observer looking at his or her self from a vantage point different than that which sees duality?

Time and the parts of the self

Let’s go back to your two pieces of mirror for a moment and arrange them once again so that you have three reflections of yourself perfectly aligned in their surfaces. Can you see each of these perspectives represented in your reflections?

I am calling the reflection to the left the traveler. Here you are staring straight down the direction we have defined as past, precluding all others, and in this example of mirrors are looking at everything seen in the mirror outside of yourself. If you become isolated in this perspective, you are literally lost in the reflections of time (and space for that matter). How about the shape-shifting reflection of yourself formed by the crossing of planes straight ahead in the direction I have called present? Is this a reflection of yourself you could interpret from the illusion in your mirrors? Now looking to the right, if I call this direction future, can you see this reflection of yourself? Are you looking at yourself in the mirror with the awareness that you are obscuring two other reflections? Can you search this plane of the mirror and see these two other reflections of yourself? This is yet another example of the basis for the entire deck of cards and potentially representative of what we are trying to define as parts to a self.

If you are a self somehow constructed of three parts, how might you recognize these parts? Is there a way for the you that you know as yourself to tune into your own subconscious resources? Could it be that the parts of you function in ways that are distinctly different? Ways in alignment with your ability to perceive reality itself? Is there any one way to define these parts of us, or is such precise definition elusive in a paradox? Do mental constructs only take us so far? Could it be that certain understandings only come about from experience? By actually having the experience of hearing from your own estranged voices? Most likely so is it not? Difficulties aside, we are going to try. I suggest that these parts of you talk from understandable perspectives and that by listening to the perspective from which one speaks, we can tune in to the part of them from which we are hearing.

37
